sections.title: 📖 What is the Cartesian Coordinate System?
sections.content:
The Cartesian coordinate system is a way to describe the position of a point in a plane using two numbers: (x, y).

It was invented by the French mathematician René Descartes (1596-1650).
keyPoints: Coordinates: (x, y) where x is horizontal distance and y is vertical distance

sections.title: 🔲 The Four Quadrants
sections.content: The axes divide the plane into four regions called quadrants:
points: Quadrant I: (+, +) → Both x and y are positive
Quadrant II: (-, +) → x is negative, y is positive
Quadrant III: (-, -) → Both x and y are negative
Quadrant IV: (+, -) → x is positive, y is negative

sections.title: 📐 Distance Between Two Points
sections.content: The distance between two points (x₁, y₁) and (x₂, y₂) is calculated using the distance formula:

formulas.name: Distance Formula
formulas.description: This is derived from the Pythagorean theorem

sections.title: 📍 Midpoint Formula
sections.content: The midpoint of a line segment connecting two points is the average of their coordinates:

formulas.name: Midpoint Formula
formulas.description: The point exactly halfway between two points

sections.title: 📈 Slope of a Line
sections.content: The slope describes the steepness and direction of a line:

formulas.name: Slope Formula
formulas.description: Rise over run — vertical change divided by horizontal change
